description:
Insert a new contact.

This function allows the user to create a new contact with the provided
contact information. It will open the contact creation interface with
pre-filled information based on the provided data.

contact_info.description: A dictionary containing the contact details to insert. Possible keys include 'name', 'phone', 'email', 'address', etc.

returns.description: A content URI as a string pointing to the newly created contact. Returns None if the operation is canceled or fails.

examples:
# Insert a new contact with name and phone number
new_contact_uri = ACTION_INSERT_CONTACT({"name":  "John Doe", "phone":  "123-456-7890"})

next_turn_plans.reason: Allows the user to view the details of the newly added contact immediately if they want to check it.

example.query: Insert new contact Maria with address 5 Elm St.
example.device_response: Contact Maria added with address 5 Elm St. (content://contacts/people/maria)

next_turn_plans.reason: If a phone number was added to the new contact, the user might want to call that number right after adding it.
If the user wants to send a text message to the number they just added, this can happen immediately after adding the contact.

example.query: Insert new contact Emily with phone 010-5555-1212
example.next_turn_query: Text her saying 'Hi Emily, I just saved your number!'
example.rewrited_query: Text 010-5555-1212 saying 'Hi Emily, I just saved your number!'

arguments.message: Hi Emily, I just saved your number!

next_turn_plans.reason: If the user wants to email the newly added email address right away, this follows naturally.

example.query: Insert new contact John with email john@example.com
example.device_response: New contact John with email john@example.com created (content://contacts/people/john)

next_turn_plans.reason: Right after adding the contact, the user might continue editing it to add any missing information.
If an address was included in the contact, the next step could be to search that address in a map app to view the location.

example.query: Add contact Noah with address 77 Pine St.
example.device_response: Contact Noah added with address 77 Pine St. (content://contacts/people/noah)

description:
View a contact's details.

This function opens the contact specified by the given content URI and displays its details (name, phone, email, etc.).

contact_uri.description: The content URI of the contact to view.

returns.description: A URI as a string pointing to the contact viewed or its details (name, phone, email, etc.), or null if the viewing was unsuccessful.
example: Displaying lee's contact information: 784-2135-9871; lee@gmail.com. provided uri: content://contacts/people/lee
showing kim's uri: content://contacts/people/kim, phone_number: 880-3210-0999; email: kim@samsung.com

next_turn_plans.reason: After viewing the contact’s phone number, a natural next step is to call that number.
